title: Occurrence Script Container
text: Occurrence Script Container Code container for defining occurrence-level operations and triggers. Description The Occurrence Script code container can contain: Constant declarations with container scope System triggers Interactive triggers Operations Entries Functions Before you can activate occurrence operations, you must first do the following: Ensure that the Occurrence Interface Name for the model entity. Enter ProcScript for your occurrence operations in the Occurrence Script container of the modeled entity. Compile the entity operation signatures for the entity. Use the /ceo command line switch. To call an operation in this container, obtain a collection handle using $occhandle , then use this handle to activate the desired operation. Related Links Script Container Location for defining the ProcScript modules of the associated object. Operations Operations are ProcScript modules that are analogous to methods in other programming languages; they perform specific actions and can be activated by other ProcScript modules, and even by non-Uniface components. Collection Script Container Code container of an entity that is used for operations that handle the collection of entity occurrences (as opposed to individual occurrences). Handles A handle is a reference to an object, such as a synchronous component instance, entity, occurrence, or field. Handles can be used to call operations on the referenced object, or to pass these objects as variables and parameters. Public and Partner Operations Operations can be modified using the public or partner modifier. Public operations can be invoked from other components. Partner operations can only be invoked from the component in which they are defined. $occhandle Returns the handle of the current occurrence of the specified entity. /ceo Compile entity collection operations and occurrence operations.
